generated_pair.question: What types of commands does the Get-Command cmdlet retrieve by default when no parameters are specified?
generated_pair.answer: By default, Get-Command gets all the cmdlets, functions, and aliases installed on the computer.
supporting_sentences: Without parameters, Get-Command gets all the cmdlets, functions, and aliases installed on the computer.

generated_pair.question: What parameter can be used with Get-Command to retrieve only the commands imported into the current session?
generated_pair.answer: The ListImported parameter can be used to get only the commands in the current session.
supporting_sentences: To get only commands that have been imported into the current session, use the ListImported parameter.

generated_pair.question: What does the -All parameter do when used with the Get-Command cmdlet?
generated_pair.answer: The -All parameter gets all commands with the specified name, including commands of the same type that have the same name, and returns them in execution precedence order.
supporting_sentences: The All parameter is useful when there is more than one command with the same name in the session.
With the All parameter, Get-Command gets all commands with the specified name and returns them in execution precedence order.

generated_pair.question: What type of objects does Get-Command return for executable files found in the PATH environment variable?
generated_pair.answer: Get-Command returns an ApplicationInfo object (System.Management.Automation.ApplicationInfo) for each executable file.
supporting_sentences: It returns an ApplicationInfo object (System.Management.Automation.ApplicationInfo) for each file, not a FileInfo object (System.IO.FileInfo).

generated_pair.question: Which parameter of Get-Command allows filtering commands by the type of parameter they accept?
generated_pair.answer: The ParameterType parameter allows filtering commands by the type of parameter they accept.
supporting_sentences: Specifies an array of parameter names. This cmdlet gets commands in the session that have parameters of the specified type. Enter the full name or partial name of a parameter type. Wildcard characters are supported.
